name: PHYS_MEM_MAP_PORT_IO_Dell_DBUtilDrv2_shape
notes: Detects the Dell DBUtilDrv2 / dbutil_2_3 BYOVD primitive shape: arbitrary phys-mem map via MmMapIoSpace + arbitrary port-IO via DPC-deferred __outbyte/__outword/__outdword. Sequence (1) is the MmMapIoSpace caller-controlled prelude (xor r8d,r8d CacheType=MmNonCached, mov rdx,rbp Length, mov rcx,rax PhysAddr, IAT-call, NULL-check, fallthrough to mov eax,0xCxxxxxxx NTSTATUS — wildcarded to cover both 0xC00000A0 STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES and sibling 0xCxxxxxxx error tails seen across the family). Sequence (2) is the MmUnmapIoSpace tail with the SSE2 zeroing pattern (movdqu xmm6,[rdx]) used to scrub the mapping descriptor on close. Sequence (3) is the canonical DPC port-IO worker prologue (push 7x regs + mov rbp,rcx + call shared-fence), occurring at all four __outdword/__outword/__outbyte/SMI-trigger DPC entry points — min 2 hits filters single-worker drivers. Sequence (4) is the SMI / APM IO-port fence (in al,0x81 ; out 0x81,al — DMA page register read-modify-write used here as a serializing barrier around the OUT to port 0xB2). The import_required quintet pins the BYOVD phys-mem + DPC-port-IO architecture; the import_forbidden gate rejects any properly hardened driver that uses ProbeForRead/Write, SeAccessCheck, SePrivilegeCheck, ExGetPreviousMode, or IoCreateDeviceSecure. negative_strings reject drivers carrying SDDL ACL strings ('D:P(' / 'SDDL'), explicit PreviousMode/AccessCheck handling, or storage-port markers (StorPort/ScsiPort) which match the phys-mem shape but are not weaponizable. positive_strings DBUtil / DBUtilDrv2 confirm the specific Dell-DBUtil lineage when present; MmMapIoSpace / MmAllocateContiguousMemorySpecifyCache strings cover the broader sibling family. Reference: dbutil_2_3.sys at mj14=0x11170, device_type=0x9b0c, 15 IOCTLs in switch routed off LowPart of Parameters.Read.ByteOffset; MmMapIoSpace gate at ioctl 0x9b0c1f40 sub_15100, port-IO at 0x9b0c1f80..8C / 0x9b0c1fc0..c4 sub_15008, DPC SMI/OUT workers sub_116EC/sub_1167F/sub_11657/sub_11630; trivial pointer-equality gate (input_buf[+0x10] == SystemBuffer ptr). Public CVE writeups conflate DBUtil with MSR-R/W; the 0F 30 / 0F 32 opcodes are absent from this binary — primitive class is PHYS_MEM_MAP + PORT_IO, not MSR_ACCESS.
